q: Heat is a form of ______.
Heat can be transferred from one system to another due to a ______ difference.
Heat transfer and thermodynamics are two ______ branches of science.
Heat transfer is a discipline of ______.
______ Engineering concerns the generation, use, conversion, and exchange of thermal energy between physical systems.
Thermodynamics deals with ______ states by outlining the relationship between energy and work.
One of the basic requirements for heat transfer is the presence of a ______.
There can be no net heat transfer between the two systems at the same ______.
In fluids, heat is mainly transferred by ______.
In heat ______ the motion of fluid itself carries heat from one place to another.
In solid, heat is transferred by ______.
In heat ______ process, heat energy is transferred among the adjacent parts of the material without the motion of the material itself.
In the heat ______ process, heat is transferred in the form of electromagnetic waves.
The individual parts of a medium do not move as a whole in heat ______.
Heat is transferred by successive distribution among the adjacent parts in heat ______.
When heat is transferred via conduction, heat energy is transferred internally by ______ of atoms and molecules.
When a teaspoon is put into a hot cup of tea, the spoon which acts as a ______ for heat transfer, does not move at all.
The rate of heat conduction is ______ proportional to the temperature difference.
The rate of heat conduction is ______ proportional to the cross-sectional area of the conducting medium.
The rate of heat conduction is ______ proportional to the thickness of the conducting medium.
The SI unit of thermal conductivity is ______.
The ratio of the temperature difference to the length is called the ______.
The unit of the temperature gradient is ______.
The temperature gradient is also expressed in differential form as ______.
The rate of heat flow per unit area is called heat ______.
The unit of heat flux density in the SI units is ______.
The unit of heat current in the SI units is the ______.
The ______ conductivity of a material is the rate of heat transfer per unit area per unit temperature gradient.
A high value for thermal conductivity indicates the material is a good thermal ______.
A low value for thermal conductivity indicates that the material is a ______ conductor or thermal insulator.
A low value for thermal conductivity indicates the material is a thermal ______.
Materials that are poor conductors of heat have ______ thermal conductivity values.
Heat ______ is the flow of heat through a fluid.
Heat ______ is the flow of heat through a fluid by the movement of the fluid itself.
In heat ______ the transfer of energy occurs between an object and its environment due to fluid motion.
In heat ______ the heat is carried by a flowing fluid from one place to another.
When rooms are heated by fire, heating is done by ______.
When rooms are heated by fire, the ______ in the room carries the heat.
The ______ force is what drives natural convection.
The ______ difference between the air heated by the heat sources and the surrounding ambient air drives the hot air upwards and away from the heat sources.
Some of the weather conditions are created by heat ______.
The heat loss by ______ is important for living beings.
The household hot water system is designed based on the process of heat ______.
In ______ countries, many homes have a hot water tank.
In the household hot water system, hot water from the ______ rises and flows into the tank.
When we use a hot water tap at home, we draw hot water from the ______ of the tank.
Heat ______ is a transfer of heat that does not require a material medium.
In heat radiation, heat is transferred by ______.
The transfer of energy by ______ does not require an intervening medium.
The transfer of energy by radiation does not require the ______ medium.
The speed of heat radiation in a vacuum is ______.
The energy of the sun reaches the earth by heat ______.
The bodies emit thermal radiation because of their ______.
All bodies at a temperature above ______ emit thermal radiation.
Radiation is mostly considered to be a surface phenomenon for solids that are ______ to thermal radiation.
An object which can absorb all the electromagnetic radiation falling upon it is a ______.
The radiation emitted by a ______ is called the blackbody radiation.
The maximum rate of radiation that can be emitted from a surface area at an absolute temperature is given by ______ law.
The emissivity of an object depends to a large extent on how ______ it is.
A black object is a good absorber and radiator whose emissivity is ______.
The net energy change is the difference between the radiated energy and the ______ energy.
______ is the study of the systems involving energy in the form of heat and work.
An example of a thermodynamic system is a gas confined by a ______ in a cylinder.
Thermal ______ is an important concept in thermodynamics.
If two systems are in thermal equilibrium, there is no ______ transfer between them.
Thermal equilibrium occurs when the systems are at the same ______.
Systems at the same ______ will be in thermal equilibrium with each other.
The ______ law of thermodynamics expresses thermodynamic equilibrium.
In a thermodynamic system, the sum of the work done and the change in internal energy equals the ______.
The sign of the heat flow is ______ if heat is added to the system.
The sign of the heat flow is ______ if heat is removed from the system.
The sign of the total work done is ______ if work is done by the system.
The sign of the total work done is ______ if work is done on the system.
The first law of thermodynamics is an application of the conservation of ______ for heat and thermodynamic processes.
The first law of thermodynamics relates the change in internal energy of a system to ______ added and the work done by the system.
The ______ of a system is the sum of the energies of all the constituent particles.
The internal energy of ideal gases depends only on ______.
The internal energy of an ideal gas is directly proportional to the ______.
Heat transfer and work are both energy in ______.
The ______ process occurs at constant pressure.
An example of an ______ process is a gas being slowly heated or cooled, confined by a piston in a cylinder.
The heat, the work, and the change in internal energy are nonzero in the ______ process.
The work done by the system in an isobaric process is the ______ multiplied by the change in volume.
The isochoric process occurs at constant ______.
The work done is ______ in the isochoric system.
In the ______ system, heat flow equals the change in internal energy.
The isothermal process occurs at a constant ______.
For a process to be isothermal, any heat flow must occur ______.
For a process to be isothermal, any heat flow into or out of the system must occur slowly to maintain the thermodynamic ______ of the system.
For an ideal gas, internal energy is only a function of the ______.
For an ______ process, the work done equals the heat flow.
For the ______ process, the work done equals the heat flow.
In the ______ process, there is no heat transfer into or out of the system.
In the ______ process, the heat flow is zero.
A heat engine converts ______ to mechanical energy.
Only heat and ______ may pass across the boundaries of a heat engine.
A series of processes in which a system is brought back to its initial state is a ______.
The change in internal energy of a heat engine is ______.
For a heat engine, the ______ equals heat absorbed minus heat rejected.
The transformation of heat into work is usually accomplished in practice by ______ general types of engines.
In heat engines, a gas or a mixture of gases contained in a ______ undergoes a cycle.
In gasoline engines, the combustion of the gasoline and the air takes place explosively by the ______.
The cycle in a gasoline engine involves the performance of ______ processes.
In gasoline engines, the processes which require the motion of the piston are called ______-stroke engine.
The gasoline engine is called a ______ engine.
In the ______ stroke of the gasoline engine, a mixture of gasoline vapour and air is drawn into the cylinder.
The intake stroke of a gasoline engine is also called the ______ stroke.
In the intake stroke of the gasoline engine, the petrol-air mixture enters through the ______ valve.
In the ______ stroke of the gasoline engine, the mixture of gasoline vapour and the air is compressed until its pressure and temperature rise considerably.
In the ______ process of the gasoline engine, the spark plug fires and ignites the mixture.
For a typical compression ratio of 8 and with $\gamma=1.4$, the theoretical efficiency of a gasoline engine is ______.
In a diesel engine, when the piston is at the top of its travel, the cylinder contains highly ______ air.
In a diesel engine, the ______ fuel is sprayed into the cylinder by the injector.
In a diesel engine, the fuel is ignited by the ______ heat of the compressed air.
The fuel compression is ______.
The final compression temperature is ______.
The final compression is ______.
The Carnot engine is an ______ engine.
The reversible cycle of the Carnot engine is known as the ______ cycle.
Carnot engine operates upon two ______ and two isotherms.
The Carnot cycle consists of ______ reversible processes.
The Carnot cycle consists of ______ isothermal processes.
The Carnot cycle consists of ______ adiabatic processes.
Carnot analyzed the transformation of energy during one complete cycle of the engine and determined the conditions for ______ efficiency.
Kelvin proposed the absolute thermodynamic ______ scale.
We can define thermal efficiency ______ as the ratio of the heat absorbed by the engine to the heat rejected.
